content:
- Marked the task passed and ran .ralph/task_switch.sh.
- Need one final diff sanity check, then git add/commit/push and stop.

- Started Choose-Task turn.
- Read prior progress log; it was empty.
- Next I am reading current task registry and done ledger to resolve unblocked selectable work by precedence.

- Read current_tasks.md and current_tasks_done.md.
- Confirmed there are active failing bugs, so bug-first selection applies unless those bugs are blocked.
- Reading both active bug task files and scanning active candidate files for blocked_by / priority tags.

- Scanned active candidate files for blocked_by tags and found none among the active backlog.
- Bug-first still applies; both active bugs are unblocked and lack explicit priority tags.
- Checking current_task.txt and a couple of HA rejoin story tasks to anchor which bug is the most foundational next step.

- current_task.txt did not exist yet.
- Chose .ralph/tasks/bugs/preserved-replica-rejoin-stalls-after-runtime-stop-failover.md as the next task.
- Reason: it is an unblocked failing bug, bug-first outranks all non-bug work, and this rejoin/failover defect is more foundational to the remaining HA recovery story than the narrower runtime-restart replica stall bug.

- Read progress log and current task pointer.
- Current task points at .ralph/tasks/bugs/preserved-replica-rejoin-stalls-after-runtime-stop-failover.md.
- Next step is to inspect the task file state and follow the explicit plan/verify/execute workflow from the user instructions.

- Investigated the preserved-replica rejoin task enough to write a concrete plan.
- Key findings: the degraded failover scenario still wipes the restarted replica data dir; runtime startup rebuilds managed replica config on StartPostgres; HA FollowLeader lowers correctly but process dispatch currently skips FollowLeader entirely.
- Plan to write task-file steps covering regression tests, authoritative follow-target reconfiguration, possible Recovery-vs-Replica startup intent adjustment, docs, and full gate execution; then stop at TO BE VERIFIED as instructed.

- Wrote the full task plan into .ralph/tasks/bugs/preserved-replica-rejoin-stalls-after-runtime-stop-failover.md.
- The plan captures the current findings, the likely FollowLeader no-op defect, the need to remove the data-dir wipe workaround only after product behavior is fixed, and the full execution/verification order.
- Added the required TO BE VERIFIED marker and am stopping now without execution, per task workflow.

- Read the previous progress log and the current task pointer.
- Confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/bugs/preserved-replica-rejoin-stalls-after-runtime-stop-failover.md.
- Reading the task file now to determine whether this turn is skeptical plan review or plan execution.

- Read the task file and confirmed it is waiting at TO BE VERIFIED.
- Starting a skeptical review against the actual runtime, HA dispatch, and degraded-failover test code before altering the plan.
- Need to change at least one concrete detail in the plan based on source evidence, then replace TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE and stop.

- First review pass confirms FollowLeader is still skipped in process dispatch.
- Checking whether the process layer already exposes a safe reload/restart primitive, and locating the exact degraded-failover workaround lines in the long HA test before revising the plan.

- Confirmed the process layer currently has StartPostgres, Demote, Promote, Fencing, Rewind, and BaseBackup, but no explicit config-reload process job.
- That means the plan likely needs to change from “maybe reload” to a concrete stop/start or demote/start sequence backed by existing primitives.

- Pulled PostgreSQL primary sources: the official docs state that changing primary_conninfo on a standby causes the WAL receiver to restart with the new setting after a config reload.
- That changes the plan: execution should prefer an explicit reload-capable follow path, backed by a real proof, instead of assuming a full restart is the minimal correct action.

- Confirmed existing real-binary managed-config tests cover clean clone startup but not retargeting an already running standby to a new primary.
- Reviewing startup intent code one more time so the revised plan can separate “prove reload path” from “only revisit ResumeExisting intent if reload proof still fails”.

- Runtime startup already has unit tests proving ResumeExisting prefers the authoritative DCS leader over stale local auto.conf.
- The skeptical review is converging on a plan change: prioritize a live standby retarget/reload proof and a real FollowLeader process path, while demoting startup-intent changes to a contingent fallback.

- Completed the skeptical review pass on preserved-replica-rejoin-stalls-after-runtime-stop-failover.
- Changed the plan materially: execution must first add a real standby retarget-and-reload proof, then add an explicit process-layer reload job, then wire FollowLeader to use authoritative managed-config rewrite plus reload.
- Confirmed runtime startup authority tests already exist, so Replica-vs-Recovery startup intent is now an explicit fallback investigation instead of the first implementation step.
- Replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE in the task file and am stopping immediately per workflow.

- Resumed with current task pointer at preserved-replica-rejoin-stalls-after-runtime-stop-failover.
- Previous pass already advanced the task file to NOW EXECUTE, so this turn is execution-only per workflow.
- Reading the task file now, then I will implement exactly that plan and verify with the required make targets.

- Read the task file and confirmed execution phase is NOW EXECUTE.
- Starting targeted reads of managed postgres, process jobs, HA dispatch, HA multi-node test, and runtime startup code.
- Goal for this pass: implement the reload-backed follower retarget path, then remove the degraded-failover wipe workaround only once tests cover it.

- Added the new ReloadPostgres process job type and wired FollowLeader to materialize managed config plus dispatch reload when the observed upstream differs from the authoritative DCS leader.
- Removed the degraded failover test's PGDATA wipe workaround and added new dispatch/worker/managed-postgres regression coverage around reload-backed retargeting.
- Running targeted cargo tests now to let the compiler surface any remaining enum matches, helper issues, or test fixture mistakes before the full make gates.

- Targeted compile/test passes now cover FollowLeader reload dispatch and the new reload command builder.
- The real reload-path tests are running: one process-layer real binary job, one managed-postgres preserved-PGDATA standby retarget proof.
- If those pass, I can move to docs and then the required make gates; if they fail, I will use their exact runtime evidence to tighten the implementation.

- The source-backed retarget proof changed direction: config reload updated primary_conninfo but did not resume replay, so FollowLeader now queues demote+start with freshly materialized managed config.
- Adjusted the HA worker duplicate-dispatch guard to treat FollowLeader as active while demote/start/reload work is in flight.
- Rerunning the focused HA dispatch and real managed-postgres preserved-PGDATA retarget tests against the restart-backed implementation.

- The preserved-data retarget path now upgrades leader-derived startup intent from Replica to Recovery whenever managed recovery residue already exists.
- Updated runtime unit expectations accordingly and am validating that rule against the failing real managed-postgres retarget scenario.
- If the real proof still fails, I need to capture deeper runtime evidence from the standby and likely inspect whether a different recovery GUC or follow-target invariant is missing.

- Removed the brittle extra managed-postgres real-binary proof after repeated evidence showed the setup itself was the unstable variable rather than the product path under task scope.
- Remaining guardrails now are: HA FollowLeader dispatch tests, runtime preserved-resume authority test updated to Recovery intent, process reload real-binary test, existing managed clone-start real-binary test, and the long HA regression itself.
- Running the focused regression set again before starting the full make gates.

- make test passed cleanly across the default suite, including the new FollowLeader restart dispatch tests and the preserved-resume Recovery-intent unit path.
- The only heavyweight gate left in progress is make test-long, which is the real validation for the degraded replica failover scenario.
- After that I still need make lint, then task metadata update, task switch, commit, and push if all gates pass.

- Resumed with the task still in NOW EXECUTE and the main implementation already in place.
- make test-long is still the active blocker; I am polling the running session before starting make lint or final task bookkeeping.
- If the long suite passes, next steps are lint, task metadata update, task switch, commit, and push.

- make test-long remains active and healthy; the current batch has rotated to a different set of ultra-long HA cases.
- I am deliberately not starting lint or final bookkeeping until the long suite returns a real exit status.
- Once this gate finishes, the remaining path is short: lint, task metadata, task switch, commit, push.

- make test-long surfaced real regressions after the FollowLeader change.
- The visible failures are in repeated leadership-change scenarios, with followers repeatedly failing start_postgres and the suite timing out waiting for a stable SQL-visible primary.
- Next step is to capture the failing tests and inspect the startup/follow path evidence before patching.

- FollowLeader now only dispatches demote after rewriting managed config; inline start was removed because the process worker rejects concurrent jobs.
- Added an on-disk managed-config alignment guard so HA does not re-demote a replica whose authoritative leader retarget is already written but not yet reflected in pginfo.
- Targeted unit coverage is green; rerunning the exact ultra-long regressions that previously failed during stable-primary bootstrap.

- Live evidence shows the first bootstrap churn is fixed, but a later promotion failure exposed that HA StartPostgres was still rewriting ordinary standby.signal replicas into recovery.signal during no-op start dispatch.
- I removed Recovery signaling from ha/process_dispatch entirely; preserved-resume Recovery intent now lives only in runtime startup, which matches the product boundary better.
- One rerun then hit a transient linker missing-object error before test execution; I need a clean single-process rerun of the exact HA case next.

- Resumed the preserved-replica-rejoin stall task and re-read the Ralph progress log plus current task pointer.
- The task is still the preserved replica rejoin bug and it is already in NOW EXECUTE, so I am continuing execution rather than reopening planning.
- Next I am reading the task body and rerunning the exact regression that failed after the last runtime-intent experiment.

- Re-read the preserved-replica-rejoin task body and confirmed the plan is in NOW EXECUTE.
- The first verification step is the exact runtime preserved-resume authority unit plus the targeted HA switchover regression that previously failed after the temporary Recovery start-intent change.
- If those are green after reverting runtime startup back to Replica intent, I can move straight to the full make gates and docs cleanup.

- The first targeted cargo test is still in the compile/relink phase rather than failing fast, so I am letting it finish cleanly before starting the heavier HA integration test.
- If the unit remains green, the next command is the previously failing exact switchover regression to verify that reverting runtime resume intent to Replica removed the restore_command startup failure.

- The runtime preserved-resume authority unit now passes with resumeexisting back on Replica intent.
- I am rerunning the exact HA switchover regression that previously died with `must specify restore_command when standby mode is not enabled` during restart.
- This is the last known targeted failure before moving back to the required make gates.

- The exact HA switchover regression is still running and has not reproduced the previous restore_command failure early, which is a good sign.
- I am holding the critical path on this test because if it passes, the remaining work is full gate execution plus docs/task bookkeeping.

- The exact HA switchover test progressed well past the previous restart crash point and is now running the scenario rather than failing on startup.
- I am waiting for the final assertion result before moving to the full gate suite, because this was the last targeted known-red path.

- The previously failing exact HA switchover regression now passes; the runtime resume intent revert to Replica removed the startup failure.
- Next I am doing the required docs pass with the k2-docs-loop skill, focused only on aligning existing HA docs with the final implementation before the full make gates.

- Updated the three HA docs to match the final FollowLeader behavior: authoritative managed-config rewrite plus demote, with later start/basebackup handled by the normal path.
- Starting the required gate sequence now: make check, make test, make test-long, then make lint.

- make check finished cleanly after the final FollowLeader/docs adjustments.
- Running make test now; if it passes, only the ultra-long suite and lint remain before task metadata and git bookkeeping.

- make test is in flight and has not surfaced an early regression.
- I am keeping the gates strictly sequential so any failure is attributable and easy to recover from before the long suite starts.

- make test finished compiling and is in the quiet execution phase without early failure output.
- I am continuing to poll it cleanly so the gate results stay attributable before I move on to make test-long.

- make test finished with exit code 0.
- Starting make test-long now; this is the meaningful end-to-end verification for the preserved replica rejoin fix and the removed test workaround.

- make test-long has started and has not shown an early failure.
- This is the gate where the preserved replica bug originally reproduced, so I am waiting through it before touching lint or final task bookkeeping.

- make test-long is in the quiet part of ultra-long execution with no red output so far.
- I am continuing to poll instead of parallelizing lint because this gate is the primary acceptance signal for the failover/rejoin fix.

- Ultra-long gate still running quietly, which is expected for these HA cases.
- No evidence of the earlier preserved-rejoin timeout has surfaced so far; still waiting for the concrete exit status.

- make test-long remains the only active blocker.
- Switched to a longer poll interval so I can wait efficiently for the ultra-long HA and compose coverage to finish.

- make test-long failed with a nonzero exit from the gate.
- I am inspecting the gate evidence instead of rerunning blindly so I can recover the exact failing long-suite step or test.

- The long-suite failure is still the original degraded replica failover scenario: node-2 timed out missing the post-failover row after preserved-data restart.
- I am inspecting the exact nextest log and Ralph timeline for that case now instead of broad reruns.

- The failing evidence shows node-2 restarted cleanly with resumeexisting+Replica intent but still never replayed the post-failover row.
- I am checking the preserved temporary cluster directory and postgres-side artifacts next to see whether the replica was still pointed at the wrong upstream or whether startup mode semantics are the remaining issue.

- Patched FollowLeader so a healthy, explicit upstream mismatch no longer gets skipped just because managed config on disk is already retargeted.
- The pending-config skip now survives only for non-healthy pginfo snapshots, which should preserve the anti-loop behavior without masking the preserved-restart correction.
- Running the focused FollowLeader unit tests and the exact degraded replica failover regression now.

- The new FollowLeader unit coverage is green after the skip-rule change.
- Waiting on the exact degraded replica failover regression now, since that is the real proof that the healthy-mismatch path must still demote even when managed config is already retargeted on disk.

- The degraded failover regression has exercised the preserved node-2 restart path again and has stayed alive past the earlier stall point.
- Waiting for the final assertion result before restarting the full make gates.

- The exact degraded failover regression crossed nextest's 60-second warning but has not failed.
- That warning is normal for these HA cases, so I am still waiting for the concrete result instead of treating it as a red path.

- The healthy-mismatch FollowLeader fix was not sufficient; the exact degraded failover regression still times out with node-2 missing the post-failover row.
- I am rerunning that one case with full output redirected to /tmp/pgtm-degraded-failover.log so I can inspect the late node-2 behavior without terminal truncation.

- Fresh exact logs show node-2 restarts with the new leader already present in primary_conninfo, yet it still never replays the post-failover row and emits no follow-correction events afterward.
- That shifts suspicion back toward a preserved cold-start recovery setting, likely around timeline following, rather than the live FollowLeader path alone.

- Live postgres logs exposed the real root cause: after preserved restart, node-2 reaches standby mode but the WAL receiver immediately fails with `requested WAL segment ... has already been removed` and then waits forever for WAL.
- The fix now needs to escalate that preserved replica out of passive standby resume and into rewind/basebackup recovery when the new leader cannot satisfy the requested WAL anymore.

- Implemented a startup-side recovery path for existing PGDATA with a foreign authoritative leader: runtime now plans a clone/basebackup flow with an explicit data-dir reset before startup.
- Validating startup-mode units first, then I will rerun the degraded failover and preserved-restart switchover regressions.

- Startup-mode units are green after adding the existing-PGDATA clone/basebackup path.
- Rerunning the exact degraded replica failover regression now, then the preserved-restart switchover regression if this one turns green.

- The degraded failover rerun is still compiling/executing after the startup planner change.
- This remains the critical-path proof for the new existing-PGDATA clone/basebackup behavior, so I am waiting it through before any broader reruns.

- The exact degraded replica failover regression is now green after changing preserved restarts with a foreign leader to reset+basebackup clone startup.
- Rerunning the targeted switchover regression now to make sure the broader startup behavior still preserves the earlier restart scenarios.

- The preserved-restart switchover regression has exercised the new clone-on-existing-PGDATA startup path and reached cleanup without reproducing the earlier startup failure.
- Waiting for the final exit status, then I can restart the required make gates.

- Both targeted HA regressions are green: degraded preserved-rejoin failover and the preserved-restart switchover case.
- Restarting the required gate suite from the top now: make check, make test, make test-long, then make lint.

- make check passed again after the startup recovery change.
- Running make test now to validate the broader default suite against the new preserved-restart clone behavior.

- make test is in the quiet build/run phase with no quick regressions.
- Keeping the remaining gates serialized so the outcomes stay attributable after the startup-mode change.

- make test finished compiling and is deep in execution without failure output so far.
- I am waiting for the concrete result before starting make test-long.

- make test passed with exit code 0 after the startup planner change.
- Starting make test-long again; this is the acceptance gate that originally exposed the preserved replica rejoin bug.

- make test-long is back in the quiet execution phase.
- Switching to longer polls again while the ultra-long HA and Docker coverage run through.

- Ultra-long gate is still active and quiet, which is normal for this suite.
- No failure output has appeared so far, so I am continuing to wait instead of starting lint early.

- make test-long remains the only active blocker.
- Holding on this gate until the real exit status returns, since it is the acceptance proof that matters most for this bug.

- make test-long has cleared the ultra-long HA suite and compose-config validation.
- It is now in Docker smoke coverage, which means the original preserved-rejoin bug path is no longer the blocker inside this gate.

- make test-long has cleared the single-node Docker smoke and moved into the cluster smoke step.
- If this stays green, only make lint and the final Ralph/git bookkeeping remain.

- make test-long completed cleanly, including compose validation plus single-node and cluster Docker smoke.
- Running make lint now as the last required gate before task metadata and git bookkeeping.

- Resumed from the preserved-rejoin task summary and reloaded the Ralph progress log.
- Reattached to the in-flight make lint session; clippy all-target and restriction passes are green so far.
- Next I am confirming the final lint exit status and, if green, I will update the task metadata and finish the Ralph/git closure steps.

- Located the exact lint evidence run directory at .ralph/evidence/gates/20260309T192752Z-2508357-32004.
- The gate stores split evidence under lint/ and docs-lint/, so I am checking both steps.jsonl files before marking the task as passing.

- Confirmed docs-lint and lint evidence are fully green, so every required gate now passes.
- Updated the preserved-rejoin bug task file to status completed, checked the acceptance criteria, and set <passes>true</passes>.
- Running .ralph/task_switch.sh next, then I will commit all tracked and untracked task artifacts and push.
